mnemonics.title: I S D - Init, Service, Destroy
mnemonics.content:
I - Initialize resources (init)
S - Serve requests repeatedly (service)
D - Destroy and cleanup (destroy)

Think: I Serve Daily

mnemonics.title: C A S H for ServletConfig
mnemonics.content:
C - Config-specific (per servlet)
A - Available in init()
S - Servlet name & parameters
H - Holder of initialization data

mnemonics.title: A S A P for ServletContext
mnemonics.content:
A - Application-wide
S - Shared across all servlets
A - Available everywhere
P - Provides application attributes

acronyms.usage: Deprecated interface that forced servlet container to create separate instance for each request. Now discouraged due to performance issues.

acronyms.content: Service method is the main entry point that dispatches to doGet/doPost methods.

visualTips.visualization:
Draw a horizontal line: [Load]---(init)---[Ready]---(service×n)---[Unload]---(destroy)

The init() and destroy() are single points, while service() is a repeating loop in the middle.
Draw ONE servlet box with MULTIPLE arrows entering (threads). Show instance variables INSIDE the box (shared), local variables INSIDE method calls (isolated).

analogies.analogy:
init() = Restaurant opens and prepares kitchen (once)
service() = Customers arrive, kitchen cooks (repeatedly)
destroy() = Restaurant closes, staff cleans up (once)

init() = Machine turns on, heats up (once)
service() = Press button, get coffee (every request)
destroy() = Turn off, clean (once)

Instance variables = shared sugar/cream station (all customers see same state)
Local variables = each cup is separate
